steps.title: Identify the model limit
steps.content: Titan's methane mole fraction affects precipitation and humidity, but observations are too sparse and discrepant to fully constrain its range at different atmospheric levels. The IPSL model responds to that uncertainty by prescribing one uniform methane value for the lowest atmospheric level.

steps.title: Reject unsupported effects
steps.content: Choice B says future observations may let the model use a single value, but the model already uses one. Choice C claims Titan's real value is higher than the model's value, which the text never supports. Choice D blames inconsistencies on variations the model does not include.

steps.title: Choose the expected mismatch
steps.content: Because a simplified uniform methane value is used for something that affects precipitation and humidity, some mismatch between the model's simulated weather and Titan's actual weather is to be expected. The answer is A.
